questions.question_text: How many commissioners serve on the Arkansas Real Estate Commission?
questions.explanation: AREC is composed of 5 commissioners appointed by the Governor. Three must be active in real estate, one is a consumer representative, and one is an elderly consumer representative.

questions.question_text: Which of the following BEST describes the composition of AREC?
questions.explanation: AREC has 5 members: 3 must be active in real estate, 1 consumer representative, and 1 elderly consumer representative. All are appointed by the Governor.

questions.question_text: Who appoints the members of the Arkansas Real Estate Commission?
questions.explanation: All five AREC commissioners are appointed by the Governor. Three must be active in real estate, one is a consumer rep, and one is an elderly consumer rep.

questions.question_text: A newly appointed AREC commissioner has never held a real estate license. Which seat could this person fill?
distractors: Any of the five commissioner seats
None without first obtaining a license
questions.explanation: Two of the five AREC seats do not require real estate activity: one consumer representative and one elderly consumer representative. The other three seats must be held by people active in real estate.

questions.question_text: If one AREC commissioner retires, how many of the remaining four seats must be held by active real estate professionals?
questions.explanation: AREC always requires 3 of its 5 seats to be held by people active in real estate. The other 2 seats are a consumer representative and an elderly consumer representative, regardless of vacancies.

questions.question_text: What is the primary role of the AREC Executive Director?
questions.explanation: The Executive Director serves as AREC's chief executive and administrative officer, handling day-to-day operations. This person is assisted by a Deputy Executive Director.

questions.question_text: AREC has the authority to do all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.correct_answer: Set commission rates for brokers statewide
distractors: Adopt rules and regulations for licensees
questions.explanation: AREC can adopt rules, investigate complaints, and impose disciplinary actions. However, it cannot set or regulate commission rates — those are negotiated between brokers and clients.

questions.question_text: A licensee contacts AREC about a routine administrative matter. Who is MOST likely to handle this?
questions.explanation: The Executive Director is AREC's chief administrative officer and handles day-to-day operations, including routine administrative matters. Commissioners set policy but don't handle daily administration.

questions.question_text: The AREC Executive Director differs from the commissioners primarily because the Executive Director:
questions.correct_answer: Manages daily operations as a staff position
distractors: Has authority to revoke licenses alone
questions.explanation: The Executive Director is a staff position managing AREC's daily administration. Commissioners are appointed by the Governor and set policy, while the Executive Director implements it.

questions.question_text: Which statement about the AREC Executive Director is TRUE?
questions.correct_answer: The position is a staff role, not a commissioner
distractors: The position is one of the five commissioners
The Executive Director is elected by licensees
The Governor cannot remove the Executive Director
questions.explanation: The Executive Director is a staff position separate from the five commissioners. The Executive Director handles daily administration while commissioners govern policy and rulemaking.

questions.question_text: Which law does AREC primarily administer?
questions.correct_answer: The Real Estate License Law (Title 17, Ch. 42)
questions.explanation: AREC administers the Arkansas Real Estate License Law (Title 17, Chapter 42). AREC also oversees the Arkansas Time-Share Act (Title 18, Chapter 14).

questions.question_text: In addition to the License Law, which other statute does AREC administer?
questions.explanation: AREC administers both the Real Estate License Law (Title 17, Ch. 42) and the Arkansas Time-Share Act (Title 18, Chapter 14).

questions.question_text: AREC suspects a broker is mishandling client funds. Which power allows AREC to review the broker's financial records?
questions.correct_answer: Authority to examine broker records at any time
distractors: Subpoena power only during a formal hearing
Authority only after a court order is issued
Power to audit only during license renewal
questions.explanation: AREC has the authority to examine broker records and trust accounts at any time, without waiting for a formal complaint or hearing. This is a key oversight power.

questions.question_text: Which of the following is NOT a power of AREC?
questions.correct_answer: Setting local property tax millage rates
questions.explanation: AREC's powers include rulemaking, licensing, investigation, and discipline. Property tax rates are set by county and local governments, not AREC.

questions.question_text: A consumer files a complaint alleging a licensee committed fraud. AREC can do all of the following EXCEPT:
questions.correct_answer: Award monetary damages to the consumer directly
distractors: Investigate the complaint against the licensee
questions.explanation: AREC can investigate, hold hearings, and impose discipline (suspension, revocation, fines), but it cannot award monetary damages directly. Consumers must pursue civil remedies for damages, or use the Recovery Fund process.
